title: Smoky Sweet Uruguayan Beef Skewers
description: Juicy tandoor‑style beef skewers glazed with a creamy coconut‑lime sauce and sweet tomatoes, perfect for a post‑workout dinner.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C) or prepare a grill for a tandoor‑style heat.
Whisk together coconut cream, lime juice, olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, chili powder, cinnamon, a pinch of salt and pepper in a bowl to make the sauce.
Add the beef cubes to the sauce, ensuring they are well coated, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to marinate.
Thread the marinated beef, halved cherry tomatoes, and onion rings onto skewers, alternating to create a colorful pattern.
Heat the grill or place the skewers on a preheated baking rack in the oven and brush each skewer with a bit more sauce.
Cook for 10–12 minutes, turning once, until the beef reaches medium‑rare (135°F) or desired doneness and the tomatoes are slightly charred.
During the last 3 minutes, brush the skewers with the remaining sauce to caramelize the exterior.
Remove the skewers from heat and let rest for 5 minutes to allow juices to redistribute.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the skewers for a fresh finish.
Serve hot as a main course, optionally with a side of sautéed greens or cauliflower rice for a complete meal.
Enjoy the smoky, sweet, and creamy flavors that make these skewers a perfect post‑workout treat.
